title: Creating Elements 🧱
description: Spawn entirely new HTML elements straight out of thin air.

slides.title: The Creator 👼
slides.content:
# Thin Air

Sometimes modifying an existing HTML tag isn't enough. 

What if you are building an infinite scrolling app like Instagram or Twitter? You don't know how many posts there are. You literally have to generate beautiful HTML blocks natively in JavaScript, from scratch, and shove them onto the screen!

slides.title: createElement() 🔨
slides.content:
## Forging the Block First, we use **`document.createElement()`**. We pass it a string indicating the specific HTML tag we want to build. ```javascript // We just forged a raw, empty paragraph tag in memory! let newPara = document.createElement("p"); ``` At this stage, the `

` exists in your RAM (Memory), but it is physically invisible to the user! We haven't placed it into the DOM tree yet.

slides.title: Dress it Up 🎨
appendChild() 🔗

quizData.question: If you successfully run `let myDiv = document.createElement("div");`, why doesn't the user see it?

options.text: Because it doesn't have an ID
Because it has no background color
Because it only exists in system Memory until you formally `.appendChild()` it into the visual document tree
Because createElement doesn't work on divs

quizData.explanation: Creating an element just builds it inside your computer's RAM. To make it leap onto the visual screen, it must be officially adopted by an existing parent DOM node using appendChild().

slides.title: Chapter Summary! 🎉
slides.content:
## You Finished Chapter 9!

You're a true Web Developer now!

You've learned:
* The **DOM** is a tree of Objects representing your HTML.
* You can target items using **`document.querySelector()`**.
* You can alter text and raw HTML using **`.textContent`** and **`.innerHTML`**.
* You can listen to user mouse clicks via **`.addEventListener("click")`**.
* You can summon data from thin air via **`document.createElement()`**.

Up Next: The final boss. Chapter 10, where we master Advanced architecture like Promises, Async/Await, and fetching server data! 🌐
